commit.message:
fix: use CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR instead of pwd in hooks

The task-completed hook used $(pwd) to find the project root, but
Claude Code doesn't guarantee CWD is the project root for hook
processes. This caused ALL 10 post-task steps (learn, trajectory,
evolve, patterns, postmortem, improve, benchmark, dashboard,
register, auto-commit) to silently fail via || true.

Fix: use ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$(pwd)} which reads the authoritative
env var Claude Code provides, falling back to pwd for manual testing.

Applied to both task-completed and session-start hooks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Model.phase_native set from CLI flag
- Output corrector created in VarMap when phase_native=true
- Forward path branches: lm_head (standard) or dot product (phase-native)

(vs 166K without phase-native). Forward path active, loss dropping.

Logistic plateau fix + three validated trajectory insights seeded for learning layer. institution_trajectories rebuilt clean.

classify_regime() now detects plateau as a distinct regime for logistic fits:
abs(final-third slope) < 5% of series mean. Bentley/Harvard enrollment correctly
classified as 'plateau' / 'plateaued along an S-curve'. MIT tuition revenue
stays 'growth' (S-curve still ascending). build_trajectory_summary() uses
'plateaued' direction verb when regime=plateau.

Three seed insights written to ingestion/learning/seed_insights.py:
BC tuition exp R²=0.99 (high), MIT tuition logistic R²=0.96 (high),
Northeastern structural break 2012 + rolling 10yr exp R²=0.79 (medium).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Candle tier: learnable ODE params via VarMap + autograd

Alpha, beta, gamma_raw now in VarMap — candle's autograd computes
gradients through the full 16-step RK4 forward automatically.
No manual backward code needed.

GpuOdeParams::learnable() creates VarMap tensors with Init values.
gamma_raw passes through tensor_softplus() (autograd-compatible)
before use in the derivative. Gradient flows through softplus,
through all 16 RK4 steps, through the neighbour coupling stencil.

Performance: 2.3s/iter (vs 842ms frozen). The autograd computation
graph for 16×4=64 derivative evaluations adds overhead. VRAM: 3.2GB
(vs 1.3GB frozen). The gradient is FREE but not cheap — future work
could use a CustomOp with cached forward for manual backward.

Loss at 100 iters: 2.84 (same as frozen — ODE is learnable but
hasn't diverged from init values yet at 100 iters).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Agentic search: FTS5 items index, unified search MCP tool, search guidance

Items (tasks/records) were completely unsearchable. Added FTS5 index on
items.title + items.description, two new MCP tools (search_items and
unified search), agentic search instructions in agent system prompts,
and frontend search modal support for item results.

Spike determined vector search unnecessary for this scale — adopted the
Claude Code pattern of iterative keyword search with LLM-driven query
reformulation instead.

commit.message:
Include active positions in PnL — premium is received upfront

European options pay premium at trade execution, so active (unsettled)
positions should count in both accrual and cash basis views. Previously
only expired positions were included, which underreported monthly
premium (e.g. showing 8.4k instead of 18k for April when only one
expiry had settled). Return rate now correctly excludes active positions
from its calculation since they haven't settled yet.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

go straight to collect_name instead of showing confirm_plan step.

Also handle paymentReady + clientSecret from backend to show Stripe
form inline after name is captured, instead of sending payment link.

feat: add 5 automated quality improvements to paper SDD framework

Implement automated validation pipeline based on deep-research-report analysis:

1. JSON Schema validation for paper/metadata.yaml (schemas/ + validate-metadata.py)

commit.message:
Candle tier: dense out_proj support (groups=1)

OutProj enum replaces BlockDiagonalLinear — supports both dense (groups=1)
and block-diagonal (groups>1). Dense uses standard candle_nn::Linear.
BlockDiagonalLinear retained as type alias for backward compatibility.

Candle at 384-dim dense: 1,091,520 params, 842ms/iter.
Full architecture parity with CPU/wgpu for the ODE + out_proj path.

Benchmark (384-dim, 8H, 6L, dense out_proj, RK4-16):
  CPU:    1,385ms/iter (1.0x)
  wgpu:   1,035ms/iter (1.34x)
  Candle:   842ms/iter (1.64x) ← fastest

commit.message:
fix: all hook scripts self-resolve Python import path

Added sys.path.insert to all 23 hook Python files so they find
sibling modules regardless of working directory. This fixes the
task-completed hook failing to run postmortem/improve steps when
the plugin runs in a non-dynos-work project (e.g., LiftingApp).

Before: PYTHONPATH had to be set manually or scripts had to run
from the hooks/ directory.

After: Each script resolves its own directory via __file__ and
adds it to sys.path. Works from any cwd, any project, no env
vars needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: dynos-work
repository.description: A Claude Code plugin that checks its own work, learns from mistakes, and fixes your code while you sleep.

commit.message:
Candle tier: RK4-N ODE replacing perturbative approximation

Replace single-step perturbative ODE with full RK4-N integration matching
CPU/wgpu tiers. All operations are Candle tensor ops (GPU-native, autograd-
compatible). The backward gradient flows through the actual RK4 computation
automatically via candle's autograd — no manual backward code needed.

kerr_derivative() computes the Kerr nonlinearity using the same pad+narrow
pattern for neighbour sums (GPU-friendly, no loops). kerr_ode_gpu() runs
N RK4 steps with proper k1/k2/k3/k4 evaluations.

GpuOdeParams updated: stores gamma (softplus), omega, alpha, beta as
tensors + rk4_steps count. Added update_coupling() for learnable params.

Benchmark at 384-dim: 911ms/iter (1.52x faster than CPU's 1385ms).
Perturbative was 520ms but ran a simpler model. RK4 is the correct
architecture match.

Root cause: ambiguous catalog format and weak instructions.

Claude now sees on every session start:

   fast-track plan-audit skip enabled. learned agents available."

This ensures Claude knows about improvements from the start,
not just when it happens to call the router during execution.

commit.message:
Minimize public API exports

Reduce public API surface to only what's needed for programmatic access:

Changes in mapper/__init__.py:
- Remove direct class imports (Neo4jConnection, GraphLoader, NameResolver, UnresolvedName)
- Remove internal modules from __all__: ast_parser, config_manager, name_resolver,
  query_system, status_checker, type_inference, setup_orchestrator
- Keep only: analyser, graph, graph_loader, __version__
- Follows Python style guide: import modules, not classes

Changes in query_system/queries/__init__.py:
- Remove individual query modules from __all__ (dead_code, module_centrality, critical_functions)
- Keep only BUILTIN_QUERIES (used by registry)
- Query modules are internal implementation details

Why minimize public API:
- Smaller surface area = easier to maintain
- Clearer distinction between public/private
- Reduces risk of breaking changes
- Users interact via CLI, not direct imports
- Internal modules can evolve without breaking external code

Users can still access all functionality via:
- CLI commands (primary interface)
- Documented public modules: mapper.analyser, mapper.graph, mapper.graph_loader

All 212 tests passing, linting clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: build LID→Phone map from history sync, contacts, and session files

WhatsApp uses LID (internal IDs) for label associations. Phone number
mappings arrive passively from multiple sources. Now we collect them all:

- messaging-history.set event (contacts with lid+phoneNumber)
- contacts.upsert and contacts.update events
- Session directory lid-mapping/*.json files on disk
- Baileys signalRepository.lidMapping.getPNForLID()
- Key store lid-mapping with multiple key formats

resolveContactPhone uses 3 methods + caching in lidToPhone map.
Returns null instead of garbage IDs when unresolvable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add OutputFormat enum and convert string literals

Add OutputFormat enum to replace string literals for query output formats:

Changes in formatters.py:
- Create OutputFormat enum with TABLE, JSON, CSV values
- Update get_formatter() to accept OutputFormat | str
- Convert string to enum automatically for backwards compatibility
- Update match statement to use enum values

Changes in cli/queries.py:
- Import OutputFormat enum
- Validate format string and convert to enum early
- Use enum throughout function instead of strings
- Convert if/elif chain to match/case for output handling

Why enum over strings:
- Type safety: catch invalid formats at compile time
- Autocomplete in IDEs
- Clearer intent in code
- Prevents typos ("talbe" vs "table")
- Follows pattern from Severity enum

Python 3.10+ compatibility:
- Uses (str, Enum) pattern instead of StrEnum (3.11+)
- Same pattern as Severity enum
- Maintains string compatibility for serialization

All 212 tests passing, linting clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add typewriter, animated counters, command palette, scroll progress

- TypewriterText: cycles through 5 role titles with typing/deleting animation
- AnimatedStats: number counters count up from 0 when scrolled into view
- CommandPalette: ⌘K spotlight with keyboard nav (↑↓ enter esc), searches
  all pages, projects, GitHub, LinkedIn, and resume download
- ScrollProgress: 2px gradient bar at top tracking scroll position
- CmdKHint button in navbar triggers the palette

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: close the full learning-to-action loop

Before: improvement engine wrote proposals but most weren't actionable.
Model recommendations and prevention rules were logged for "manual review"
but nothing auto-applied them or made the router consume them.

After:
- Model recommendations auto-apply to policy.json model_overrides
  (router already reads model_overrides)
- Prevention rules auto-apply to prevention-rules.json
  (router loads them and includes in executor plans)
- All 4 proposal types now auto-applicable:
  adjust_policy, adjust_model_policy, add_prevention_rule, seed_learned_agent

Verified end-to-end: improvement cycle applied 5 changes,
router now returns model=haiku for non-security auditors (was default),
and includes 4 prevention rules in executor plans.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Phase 11: Polish + hardening — crash recovery, approval lifecycle, overnight summaries, E2E testing

Crash recovery: autonomous tasks resume from task list state after restart instead
of being marked failed. Three-pass recovery (autonomous → sub-agents → regular).
PENDING_RESUME enum status, 2-second delayed resume on startup.

Approval lifecycle: expire_stale() wired into scheduler (60s), per-agent configurable
timeout (approval_timeout_hours), steering re-injection on approve/deny/expire,
409 guard on non-pending approvals, batch_resolve skips resolved entries.

Overnight summaries: structured run summary generation (goal, progress, tokens,
approvals, key actions), morning brief API grouped by agent, POST dismiss endpoint,
MorningBrief dashboard component.

E2E testing: 15 integration tests covering full autonomous pipeline — crash recovery,
approval flow, delegation lanes, permission inheritance, kill switch, heartbeat,
budget exhaustion, cascade termination, concurrent isolation, morning brief.

Code review: 15 findings triaged, 8 fixed (enum magic string, race condition guard,
corruption protection, GET side-effect removal, audit query expansion, steer loop
isolation, enum consistency, batch_resolve guard).

1264 tests passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rename Formatter protocol to FormatsQueryResults (verb-based naming)

Rename Formatter protocol to FormatsQueryResults following verb-based
naming convention per code-architecture.md:

Changes:
- Protocol definition: Formatter → FormatsQueryResults
- Return type annotation in get_formatter()
- Updated docstring to reflect new name

Why verb-based naming for protocols:
- Protocols describe capabilities/behaviors
- Verb-based names (FormatsQueryResults, ParsesCode) are more descriptive
- Follows established convention in python-style.md

Implementation classes (TableFormatter, JSONFormatter, CSVFormatter)
unchanged - they remain noun-based as concrete implementations.

All 212 tests passing, linting clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace bare exception handlers with specific exceptions

Replace 10 bare 'except Exception' handlers with specific exception types
following Python style guide (docs/contributing/python-style.md):

Changes by file:
- analyser/main.py: Catch OSError, UnicodeDecodeError, RuntimeError for file operations
- cli/queries.py: Catch FileNotFoundError, OSError, DriverError for query execution
- graph.py: Catch AuthError, DatabaseError, DriverError for Neo4j operations
- status_checker/checker.py (3 instances): Catch specific config/connection errors
- setup_orchestrator/orchestrator.py (4 instances): Catch DriverError, OSError, PermissionError

Test updates:
- Updated 2 tests to raise RuntimeError instead of bare Exception
- tests/integration/analyser/test_graph_storage.py: Neo4j mock failure
- tests/unit/cli/test_status.py: Config loading failure

Why specific exceptions:
- Documents what can actually go wrong
- Forces thinking about error cases
- Prevents hiding bugs (typos, AttributeErrors)
- Makes debugging easier

All 212 tests passing, linting clean.

commit.message:
Add FormulaBase engine, Formula Editor, and 200+ indicators from ArTraNew

FormulaBase engine:
- 50+ built-in functions: MA, EMA, SMA, DMA, REF, HHV, LLV, SUM,
  COUNT, STD, AVEDEV, IF, CROSS, NOT, BETWEEN, EVERY, EXIST,
  FILTER, BACKSET, EXTEND, SLOPE, CORR, LR, ZIG, SAR, and more
- FormulaData with full operator overloading (+,-,*,/,>,<,&,|,==)
- FormulaPackage output container
- FormulaParam with Double/String/Symbol/Indicator types
- Assembly registry for runtime formula discovery

Formula compiler (Roslyn):
- Runtime C# compilation to in-memory assemblies
- Error reporting with line/column mapping
- Template generation for new formulas
- CompileAndRegister for instant formula activation

Formula Editor UI:
- Code editor with Cascadia Code font, dark theme
- F5 to compile with error list (double-click to navigate)
- TreeView formula browser by category
- Method reference panel (double-click to insert)
- Save/Load .fml.cs files
- New/Compile/Apply workflow

Indicators ported from ArTraNew (9 categories):
- Bands: BBIBOLL, BOL, CDP, ENV, MIKE, SR, BBWidth, HHLLV
- Basic: MASS, STD, WAD, ZigLabel, PR, Fibonacci, LinRegr, MA, EMA, VOL
- Index: ABI, ADL, ADR, BT, CHAIKIN, MCO, OBOS, STIX
- Momentum: BIAS, CCI, DBCD, DPO, FastSTO, RSI, ROC, WR, MFI, StochRSI
- Oscillator: AD, MI, MICD, RC, RCCD, CMF, ULT, AroonOsc
- PriceVol: ASI, OBV, PVT, SOBV, WVAD
- Trend: BBI, DDI, DMA, DMI, MACD, MTM, SAR, TRIX, ADX, ICHIMOKU
- Volatility: PSY, VR, ATR

commit.message:
fix(content): readiness accuracy formula and minimum session questions (#34)

* fix(content): readiness accuracy formula and minimum session questions (#33)

Fix accuracy calculation using SUM(totalCorrect)/SUM(totalAttempts) instead
of SUM(totalCorrect)/COUNT(distinctQuestions) which could exceed 100%.
Reject sessions when domain has fewer than 5 available questions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(content): address review feedback (#33)

Add regression test proving accuracy stays ≤100% when questions are
answered multiple times (totalAttempts > questionsAttempted). Fix Karate
test to use non-existent domain (guaranteed 0 questions from cache).
Add code comment about topic-level limitation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve i.map crash, re-add CSS utilities, and add null safety to all components

Root cause: useDriverStandings/useConstructorStandings hooks were returning
the full API response object {entries:[...]} instead of just the entries array
after switching to apiFetch(). Components treating the response as an array
crashed with "i.map is not a function".

Fixes:
- Hooks now properly extract .entries from API response
- Re-added f1-gradient, f1-stripe, f1-card-glow CSS utilities
- Added Array.isArray() guards to 6 feature components:
  head-to-head, strategy-panel, pace-distribution, lap-chart,
  consistency-radar, heatmap-chart

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add API token module, multi-OIDC support, CSRF protection, and security fixes

- Add plaintext-admin-apitoken module (moved from plaintext-app) with JWT RS256 token management, user/admin/root views, and Flyway migration
- Fix OIDC config: add jackson-databind dependency, fix PrimeFaces fileUpload (sizeLimit→maxFileSize, FileUploadEvent listener)
- Support multiple OIDC providers on login page with dynamic buttons per active config
- Enable Spring CSRF protection (was disabled) with exceptions for /api/** and /oauth2/**
- Fix CVE-2020-13956: override transitive httpclient 4.5.2→4.5.14 from unirest

commit.message:
fix: force full snapshot resync for label associations

- Reset app-state-sync-version to null before resync to force WhatsApp
  to send FULL snapshot instead of just deltas
- Use isInitialSync=true so all data gets processed
- This fixes the issue where label associations (contacts per label)
  never arrived because Baileys only fetched empty patches

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: auto-improvement engine with project-local policy tuning

Added to hooks/dynopostmortem.py:
- propose_improvements(): analyzes postmortems across tasks, generates
  project-local improvement proposals (policy tuning, agent seeding,
  prevention rules, model recommendations)
- apply_improvement(): safely applies policy tuning and agent seeding
  to .dynos/ state only. Never modifies global plugin source files.
- run_improvement_cycle(): full cycle with proposal logging + application
- CLI: `propose` (dry run) and `improve` (apply safe changes)

Wired into task-completed hook as Step 5b after postmortem generation.

First run applied 2 improvements to this project:
- token_budget_multiplier: 2.0 (reduces false overrun alerts)
- fast_track_skip_plan_audit: true (low-risk tasks skip plan audit)

5 additional proposals logged for manual review (prevention rules,
model tier change).

Key design principle: improvements target project-local .dynos/ state
only, never global plugin files. Cross-project improvements go through
the ~/.dynos/ global abstraction layer separately.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Remove 10 eslint-disable comments without adding new ones

Replace return Promise.resolve() with await Promise.resolve() to
satisfy both require-await and no-useless-promise-resolve-reject.
Replace mockReturnValue(undefined) with mockReset() to avoid
no-useless-undefined. Replace mockResolvedValue(undefined) with
typed mockResolvedValue().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: auto-postmortem system generates structured analysis after every task

New: hooks/dynopostmortem.py
- Generates structured postmortem JSON + human-readable MD for each task
- Cost summary with budget comparison by (risk_level, task_type)
- Quality summary with finding counts and repair cycles
- Policy usage tracking (generic routing, fast-track, inline, discovery skip)
- Anomaly detection: token overrun, low quality, all-generic routing, high repairs
- Similar task comparison via type + domain + risk matching
- Recurring pattern detection across recent tasks (persistent issues)

Wired into task-completed hook as Step 5 (after patterns, before auto-benchmark).
Runs deterministically, no subagent spawn needed.
